title: Buildah fails to compile when btrfs headers are not installed
body:
**Description**

The `Makefile` does not build `buildah` cleanly on my RHEL 7.5 CSB without the btrfs headers installed.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```console
$ make
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```
go build -ldflags '-X main.gitCommit=6573a81 -X main.buildInfo=1536947502 -X main.cniVersion=v0.7.0-alpha1' -o buildah -tags "btrfs_noversion  containers_image_ostree_stub selinux seccomp" ./cmd/buildah
# github.com/projectatomic/buildah/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/drivers/btrfs
../../../.local/lib/go/src/github.com/projectatomic/buildah/vendor/github.com/containers/storage/drivers/btrfs/btrfs.go:8:25: fatal error: btrfs/ioctl.h: No such file or directory
 #include <btrfs/ioctl.h>
                         ^
compilation terminated.
make: *** [buildah] Error 2
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

A successful build.

**Buildah version:**

```console
$ git describe --always --dirty
6573a81
```

**Workaround:**

I needed:

```console
$ make AUTOTAGS='exclude_graphdriver_btrfs containers_image_ostree_stub selinux'
```

to build `buildah`.

**Fix:**

Looking at our tag-generating scripts, it looks like we have a local version of [libpod's `btrfs_tag.sh`][1], but are missing an analog for [its `btrfs_installed_tag.sh`][2].  Someone should bring over `btrfs_installed_tag.sh` if we want to support out-of-the-box compilation on systems without btrfs headers.

[1]: https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/v0.9.1.1/hack/btrfs_tag.sh
[2]: https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/v0.9.1.1/hack/btrfs_installed_tag.sh
